{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"I’ve got your war medals framed
   and this battered plane you made
first day apprenticed, 1912. ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Your other tools were thieved,
   but not this lump of ‘four-be-four’,
dark with your elbow-grease. ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"In the bast on your handlebars
   it had you wide-kneed, whistling 
gutters for fifty years, hating it.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"At seven your mum was dead, 
   a teacher and church organist, 
you had her slender hands, they said. ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"So soon as you could fend her clout 
   At twelve your step-ma had you at work 
At sixteen, you got to hell out ","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"to Flanders. At last I grasp 
   your pride that my hands 
are white and soft upon this page.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"document"}
